steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says trial results “sometimes fail to ______ conclusions that practitioners reach.” The explanation is that practitioners overlook confounding variables, so their conclusions are not verified by the trials. The blank needs a word meaning ‘confirm’ or ‘support.’

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — implement: means to put into action; trials don’t ‘implement’ conclusions — this is the wrong relationship. ✗
  • Choice B — circumvent: means to get around or avoid; ‘fail to circumvent’ would mean the trials successfully avoided the conclusions, which is not the meaning the passage needs. ✗
  • Choice C — corroborate: means to confirm or support. Trials that ‘fail to corroborate’ practitioners’ conclusions means the trial evidence does not back up what practitioners observed — exactly the divergence the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice D — disseminate: means to spread or distribute widely; trials don’t ‘disseminate’ conclusions — this is irrelevant to the meaning. ✗
That is the correct answer.
